chunks.text:
Repeal of the UK’s Video-Sharing Platforms regime
Repeal of the UK’s Video-Sharing Platforms regime
Online safety
Illegal and harmful content
Information for industry
Video-sharing platform (VSP) regulation
Published:
28 July 2025
On July 25, 2025, the UK’s Video-Sharing Platforms (VSP) regime was repealed, and all notified services are now regulated under the Online Safety regime.
The VSP regime ran in the UK for four years and was the UK’s flagship online safety regulation. Following its repeal, we took a look back at its
journey
 to highlight what was achieved, as well as
5 things industry can learn from online safety regulation
in practice.
Our approach to implementing the Online Safety Act
details the rules that services must now comply with, including the
illegal harms duties
and the
protection of children duties
.
Further duties will follow as the Online Safety regime continues to be implemented.
The UK’s Video-Sharing Platforms regime – A retrospective
The UK Video-Sharing Platforms regime was repealed 25 July 2025.
Check how to comply with the illegal content rules
Our interactive tool will help providers of user-to-user and search services to understand how to comply with the illegal content rules.
Video-sharing platforms: Ofcom's plan and approach
How we regulated video-sharing platforms and priorities for the last stretch of the regime before it was replaced by the online safety regime.
